summary: The suite covers all five features end-to-end — data ingestion, threshold filtering (CPU-only, memory-only, OR logic, no-match), top-N ranking (both sort axes, N > list size, invalid sort property), report formatting (header, columns, empty case), and the integration pipeline. Fixture organisation with BeforeAll/$script: scope is clean, scriptblock mocks keep every test fully isolated from live system state, and test names map 1-to-1 with requirements, making failures easy to diagnose. The main weaknesses are in rigor: there are no boundary-value tests (processes exactly at a threshold are silently excluded by the -gt operator, and this is never verified), no test for a provider that returns $null (the implementation guards against it but the guard goes untested), no test for Top = 0, negative thresholds, or missing/null properties on process objects. Error-handling coverage is thin — only the invalid SortBy path is exercised. The Context block appears only once, leaving the BeforeAll/Context nesting slightly inconsistent, but this is cosmetic. Overall a solid, trustworthy suite for happy-path and common failure modes; a few targeted boundary and error tests would push it to near-complete coverage.
